description: Rules for the mind under siege: sanity erosion, stress and panic, fear responses, acquired psychological conditions, and the composure that resists them.

sanity.description: Long-arc mechanics for the erosion of a character's grip on reality.

focuses.sanity_meter: A numeric sanity score is depleted by horrific experiences and hard to restore, charting the character's slide toward madness (Call of Cthulhu's 0-99 SAN).
focuses.cthulhu_mythos_exposure: Learning forbidden cosmic truth permanently lowers the ceiling of recoverable sanity, so knowledge and stability trade off directly (Call of Cthulhu's Cthulhu Mythos skill capping maximum SAN).
focuses.temporary_insanity: Losing too much sanity at once triggers a short episode — fainting, screaming flight, hysteria — that seizes control of the character for minutes or hours (Call of Cthulhu).
focuses.indefinite_insanity: Sustained sanity loss inflicts a lasting underlying condition that persists between scenarios until treated (Call of Cthulhu indefinite insanity).
focuses.hardened_desensitization: Surviving repeated horrors of the same kind inures the character, reducing future reactions at the cost of empathy or humanity (Unknown Armies hardened notches, Delta Green adaptation).
focuses.sanity_zero_removal: Hitting the bottom of the sanity scale removes the character from play permanently, becoming an NPC or worse (Call of Cthulhu at 0 SAN).
focuses.delusions_unreliable_narrator: An unstable character's player receives false or distorted information from the GM, making their perception of the fiction untrustworthy (Call of Cthulhu 7e delusions and reality checks).
focuses.mythos_tome_study: Deliberately reading forbidden tomes trades measured sanity loss for occult skill and spells, making study a priced transaction (Call of Cthulhu tome mechanics).
focuses.madness_as_insight: Losing stability opens perception of hidden truths, granting real mechanical sight into what sane minds cannot register (KULT: Divinity Lost, where low stability pierces the illusion).
focuses.multi_axis_madness_meters: Mental damage is tracked on several parallel meters keyed to different trauma types, each with its own hardened and failed thresholds (Unknown Armies' five madness meters).

stress.description: Short-cycle accumulation of pressure that boils over into panic and lasting trauma.

focuses.stress_track: Characters accumulate stress points from danger and horror into a track that fuels or triggers worse effects when it fills (Mothership stress, Blades in the Dark stress boxes).
focuses.panic_rolls: At moments of crisis a character must roll against accumulated stress; failure triggers a panic effect from a table of freezes, flights, and breakdowns (Mothership panic checks, Alien panic rolls).
focuses.breaking_point: Each character has a defined threshold at which accumulated strain forces a psychological break and reassessment of who they are (Delta Green breaking points, Call of Cthulhu 7e).
focuses.trauma_conditions: Maxing out stress inflicts a permanent trauma trait — cold, haunted, paranoid — that reshapes how the character must be played (Blades in the Dark trauma).
focuses.stress_relief_vices: Characters shed stress by indulging a rated vice, with overindulgence carrying its own complications (Blades in the Dark vice rolls).
focuses.stress_dice_bonus: Accumulated stress adds dice or bonuses to rolls even as it raises the odds of panic, making pressure a double-edged fuel (Alien RPG stress dice).
focuses.panic_contagion: One character's panic can trigger stress or panic tests in everyone who witnesses it, letting fear cascade through a group (Alien RPG).
focuses.stress_from_failure: Failed checks automatically add stress, so incompetence under pressure compounds itself (Mothership).
focuses.sanctuary_recovery: Stress can only be meaningfully recovered in designated safe havens or downtime phases, making safety a scarce resource (downtime in Blades in the Dark, hearth and haven rules in dark fantasy designs).

fear.description: Immediate mechanical responses to frightening threats and dreadful situations.

focuses.fear_tests: Facing a frightening threat forces an immediate test; failure imposes penalties, hesitation, or refusal to approach (WFRP Fear tests, Savage Worlds fear checks).
focuses.horror_checks: Confronting the existentially wrong or grotesque tests the mind rather than the nerves, with failure leaving lingering revulsion or marks (Ravenloft horror checks as distinct from fear checks).
focuses.dread_escalation: The system ratchets tension mechanically as the story proceeds — a tower grows less stable, candles burn down — so dread physically mounts toward collapse (Dread's Jenga tower, Ten Candles' dying light).
focuses.fear_terror_distinction: Frightening things are tiered: lesser fear imposes penalties while greater terror forces flight, paralysis, or madness outright (WFRP fear versus terror).
focuses.fear_ratings_by_threat: Monsters and situations carry numeric fear ratings that scale the difficulty or consequences of resisting them (fear ratings on WFRP creatures, Savage Worlds fear penalties).
focuses.environmental_dread: Darkness, isolation, and being alone impose their own escalating fear effects independent of any visible threat (Ten Candles' darkness, survival horror isolation rules).

conditions.description: Named psychological afflictions: how they are acquired, expressed, and treated.

focuses.madness_tables: Mental breaks are resolved on random tables that assign a specific madness, from tics to full delusional episodes (Call of Cthulhu bouts of madness, Shadow of the Demon Lord insanity tables).
focuses.phobias_manias: Characters acquire specific rated phobias or manias that trigger penalties or compulsions when encountered in play (Call of Cthulhu 7e phobia and mania lists).
focuses.disorders_treatment: Acquired disorders can be reduced or cured through therapy, medication, or institutional care over downtime, at cost in time and money (Call of Cthulhu sanitarium stays and psychoanalysis).
focuses.addictions: Dependency on substances or experiences is tracked mechanically, with cravings, withdrawal penalties, and escalating dosage (Shadowrun addiction rules).
focuses.amnesia_fugues: Trauma can sever memory, with the GM controlling what the character actually remembers and revealing lost time as play unfolds (fugue states and amnesia in Call of Cthulhu).
focuses.condition_ladder_grind: Hardship inflicts conditions from a fixed escalating ladder — hungry, angry, afraid, exhausted, injured, sick — each with set effects and specific remedies (Torchbearer, Mouse Guard).
focuses.nightmares_sleep_disruption: Disturbed sleep and recurring nightmares deny recovery and impose waking penalties, tying mental state to rest (Don't Rest Your Head's exhaustion, recurring dreams in Call of Cthulhu scenarios).

composure.description: The resources and tests by which characters keep control of themselves under pressure.

focuses.willpower_tests: Resisting coercion, temptation, or supernatural influence is a discrete test of a willpower trait (World of Darkness Willpower rolls, Resolve tests).
focuses.emotional_states: Characters enter defined emotional states — melancholy, rage, elation — that carry explicit mechanical effects until resolved (Pendragon's melancholic and maddened states).
focuses.frenzy_loss_of_control: Failing a self-control test tips the character into a berserk or predatory frenzy played partly by the rules rather than the player (Vampire: the Masquerade frenzy, Werewolf rage).
focuses.social_pressure_tracks: Composure functions as a defense pool that social and mental attacks deplete, with depletion forcing concession or influence (Exalted's Resolve and Willpower under social influence).
focuses.intimacies_leverage: A character's rated attachments and principles are the levers by which persuasion works, lowering resistance when invoked (Exalted intimacies).
focuses.willpower_recovery: Composure resources refresh through rest, fulfilled convictions, or roleplaying one's nature, making self-expression mechanically restorative (Vampire: the Masquerade willpower regain).
focuses.passion_inspiration_risk: Invoking a rated passion can grant inspired near-superhuman performance on success, but failure unhinges the character into shame or madness (Pendragon passions).
focuses.hope_dice: A dwindling reserve of hope offsets despair and fuels extraordinary effort, and its loss invites despair conditions or darkness (The One Ring's Hope pool against the Shadow, Ten Candles' hope die).
